response: The internet originated as a US military research project in the 1960s, with its precursor, ARPANET, sending its first message in 1969. Key technical developments, including the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 and the invention of the World Wide Web in 1989, created its modern foundation. Subsequent innovations like web browsers, social media, and smartphones made the internet publicly accessible and ubiquitous. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly every aspect of modern life.
